patch.value: pm search keyword mode: --match-mode (and/or/exact) + default result limit (GH-181 remainder)

GH-181 reported keyword search returning 479 unranked results. Weighted relevance scoring + sorted output + matched_fields + --phrase-exact + score_threshold are already shipped, so the core complaint is fixed. Remaining asks: (1) --match-mode and|or|exact flag with AND-preference ranking for multi-word queries (boost items matching ALL terms), (2) sensible default result limit for keyword mode (currently unlimited when --limit omitted, see search.ts resolvedLimit), (3) optional --count mode (also requested in GH-157). Related: pm-cstl (min-score threshold).

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Scoping (2026-06-14): keyword-mode default limit gap confirmed at search.ts:1872 (resolvedLimit = keyword ? limit : limit ?? maxResults — keyword skips maxResults default). Remaining: --match-mode and|or|exact (AND-preference ranking), apply maxResults default in keyword mode, --count mode. Bundled into PR feat/search-relevance-filter-parity-corpus.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Starting GH-181 remaining work: --match-mode (and|or|exact) with all-terms coverage RANKING bonus as default; keyword default limit fallback to maxResults(50)+total field; --count count-only mode. Read search.ts + list.ts + register-list-query.ts + cli-contracts + tool-option-contracts + mcp/server + completion to map all wiring sites.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: DONE (GH-181 remaining). 1) --match-mode and|or|exact: or(default)=ANY-token match PLUS an all-terms RANKING bonus (ALL_TERMS_COVERAGE_BONUS=40 added in scoreDocument when distinct tokens>1 and every token matched some field — RANKING not hard filter); and=hard-filter to items where every distinct token matched (via new SearchHit.matched_all_terms internal flag, stripped from full-mode output); exact reuses documentContainsExactPhrase (same as --phrase-exact). 2) Default keyword limit: resolvedLimit now = limit ?? maxResults (resolveSearchMaxResults default 50) — keyword mode no longer returns ALL hits; added result.total (pre-limit count, surfaced only when limit truncates). 3) --count: count-only response (count_only:true, count/total=matched total, items:[]) for both compact + verbose paths, skips projecting rows. Behavior-change note: keyword default limit 50 + coverage bonus may shift order; existing 45 search-command.spec tests all still pass (small corpora, no truncation). Wiring: search.ts, register-list-query.ts, cli-contracts SEARCH_FLAG_CONTRACTS, commander-types SEARCH_COMMANDER_STRING_OPTION_CONTRACTS, registration-helpers normalizeSearchOptions, tool-option-contracts, completion zsh/fish (bash auto), docs COMMANDS+AGENT_GUIDE, contracts golden regenerated. Tests: 4 new (coverage-rank, and-filter, exact, invalid match-mode, default-limit+total, count compact+verbose). 56/56 search spec + 3380/3380 full suite green.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Shipped (PR feat/search-relevance-filter-parity-corpus): --match-mode and|or|exact (and=hard-filter all tokens, exact=phrase containment, or=default OR + ALL_TERMS_COVERAGE_BONUS ranking so multi-term queries prefer full coverage); keyword mode now applies max_results (50) default when --limit omitted (was unlimited -> GH-181 479-result complaint); result.total surfaces pre-limit count when truncated; --count count-only mode (items:[], count_only:true) for token-efficient how-many queries. 11 new tests. Verified in sandbox.

patch.value: Shipped --match-mode/--count + keyword default limit + all-terms coverage ranking (GH-181)
